content.text: Milady app development status remained a recurring topic throughout the week. Odilitime confirmed the app is live in beta with cloud access so users do not need to download anything. A contest offering BNB or SOL rewards for screenshots was announced. Cost breakdowns for running AI agents on the Milady app were shared, ranging from approximately 7.20 USD per month for one agent to 21.60 USD per month for three agents running continuously. Earlier in the week, Odilitime stated the team worked all weekend on the app and he personally jumped in to help, but could not provide a specific release date.

Odilitime provided a comprehensive overview of the ElizaOS ecosystem. ElizaOS is an open-source framework with a SaaS platform for easier deployment. He clarified that ElizaOS and DegenAI are the only two tokens minted and supported by Labs, and that ElizaOS is not a memecoin but is intended to power the Jeju chain upon launch. Projects building on or alongside it include Babylon, an AI training ground and game for prediction markets and autonomous trading, Hyperscape, a game built on the framework, and Milady, which is pushing the elizacloud SaaS platform. DegenAI is planned to work with Babylon prediction market and autonomous trading features.

Community members debated the visibility of the Milady project within the ElizaOS ecosystem, noting that most people are unaware Milady was built on Eliza. Suggestions were made for a dedicated Milady channel inside the ElizaOS Discord. Separately, detailed feedback was submitted urging the team to consolidate all project information onto a single website, as the frequent appearance of new projects and tokens creates confusion about whether they are independent or part of the ElizaOS ecosystem. Odilitime mentioned plans to potentially create a bridge room the following week.

A debate arose regarding the nature of Hyperscape and its relationship to ElizaOS. Some community members argued it is an Eliza Labs project and a core part of the ElizaOS 10-year flywheel. However, Odilitime and another member clarified that Hyperscape is not an official Eliza Labs project but rather a project being incubated by Eliza, described as somewhere between a joint venture and a personal Shaw project. Separately, Hyperscape was noted to have reached the top 4 finalists in the Nosana Hackathon.

Nosana partnership and hackathon activity was announced. Denis from Nosana introduced himself as the ecosystem and community representative, announcing their partnership with ElizaOS for the Nosana Builders Challenge starting March 25. Workshops were scheduled for March 26 and April 2 to help participants build AI agents. Additionally, ElizaBAO shared news about an AI Sprint hackathon in collaboration with BNBChain and other partners running April 1-30 with a 50,000 USD prize pool.

Babylon Demo Day was broadcast live on X during the week, shared by Odilitime across the general discussion and partners channels. The Solana Foundation Chief Product Officer also stated that 99.99% of all onchain transactions within two years will be driven by agents, bots, and LLM-based wallets and trading products, signaling strong industry confidence in AI-driven blockchain activity.

A critical security alert was shared about a supply chain attack in litellm version 1.82.8 on PyPI. The malicious package contains a .pth file that harvests SSH keys, cloud credentials, and secrets on every Python startup, and attempts lateral movement across Kubernetes clusters. Odilitime confirmed awareness of the issue. Separately, regarding ai16z token holders who missed the migration window, Odilitime stated there is no current solution but offered to add interested users to a waitlist via DM with no guarantees.

Several developer tools and projects were announced in the coders channel throughout the week. TaskBounty shipped crypto payouts supporting USDC, ETH, and SOL with a full REST API, allowing Eliza agents to autonomously browse tasks, submit work, and receive payment without human involvement. Orbis, an API marketplace for agents, was introduced with x402 pay-per-call functionality allowing agents to call APIs, pay in USDC on Base, and receive responses without subscriptions or API keys. HatcherLabs announced hatcher.host supporting ElizaOS and Milady deployments with a free trial. Ivan Jeremic announced the Pythia MCP server enabling AI agents to query live on-chain market indicators without API keys. The xProof.app plugin was shared, anchoring every ElizaOS agent decision on the MultiversX blockchain before execution.

On March 29, 2026, a new plugin proposal was submitted to add @axnetfun/plugin-axnet to the elizaos-plugins registry, and a routine dependency update was made to bump the cryptography library from version 46.0.5 to 46.0.6 in the elizaos/eliza repository. The most significant activity centered on an ongoing cross-repository discussion about the Dreamline x402 Policy Facilitator, which addresses how human operators can authorize autonomous agent payments. Contributor hermesnousagent proposed a pre-authorization layer for x402 payments including a detailed three-object contract specification. A key open question is whether the current x402 plugin provides any operator-visible event before a fetch call executes, or whether the first signal an operator receives is a wallet balance change. Community input is being actively sought on this point.
